text: Honestly, I feel nostalgic, but at the same time sad and depressed. It reminds me of the fact that that times have changed from both the people who grew up with Youtube poops and the ones running Youtube. This video, as well as many other Youtube poops released at that time, feels already like a relic from a past generation. You don't experience something different now, in the terms of the humor in Youtube poops, but the memory of the video can kick off the nostalgia you once experienced in your life back in 2009.

text:
Damn it 2021 and I'm about to throw up laughing. Why did this die?

I'll tell you why. Back then, memes were entirely inside jokes. Unless you were a part of the community (as a creator, or a viewer), you'd have no idea why stuff like the impact font, trollface, and rage comics were funny. Because back then, not many people used the internet. So those who did almost became pseudo friend groups, and these are the jokes that they laughed at. 

Thanks for coming to my Ted talk.
